title: wm-state: rebuild failure rollback
description: P0. Mid-rebuild error strands windows in wm_state_park_<ws>. No cleanup. Wrap rebuild in try; on failure, capture per-wid original workspace BEFORE park and move them back. Verify: force error mid-rebuild, assert all windows return to origin.

title: wm-state: capture + restore floating windows
description: P0. build-workspace-layout walks only `nodes`, skips `floating_nodes`. Floating windows silently dropped on save. Must capture them as state entries with `floating: true` flag; restore applies `[id=X] floating enable` after placement. Verify: craft yaml with floating entry, restore recreates as floating.

title: wm-state: restore must defer old-terminal kill until new attach
design:
## Bug
pre-clean-target kills target ws's terminals BEFORE the new respawn-flow attaches new kittys. Killing the kitty detaches its tmux client. tmux-start sets destroy-unattached on, plus tmux-cleanup hook fires on client-detached and kills any unattached non-pinned sessions in the group. Result: by the time main restore issues exec kitty -e tmux attach -t <session>, the session is gone and the new kitty's tmux attach fails. Saved tmux_window_id worthless.

## Reproducer
1. wm-state save with 3 kittys, each in its own tmux session (local_3..5).
2. wm-state restore <same ws>.
3. pre-clean-target kills the 3 kittys -> their tmux clients detach -> destroy-unattached + cleanup hook destroy local_3..5.
4. exec kitty -e tmux attach -t local_3 fails (no such session).

## Fix
Split pre-clean into two phases:
- isolate-target <name>: rename target -> <name>-old, no kill. Old terminals stay alive on <name>-old, tmux clients stay attached, sessions stay alive.
- kill-old-terminals <name>-old: walk -old ws, kill terminal windows. Run AFTER new respawn finished and new kittys attached to those sessions (so each session has the new client, destroy-unattached doesn't fire).

main restore loop:
  1. for ws: isolate-target (rename only)
  2. for ws: workspace + append_layout + exec new terminals
  3. wait for new windows mapped+swallowed (poll i3 tree, or sleep ~500ms)
  4. for ws: kill-old-terminals on <name>-old
  5. return to start_ws

Collision case (<name>-old already exists from prior restore): handle in isolate-target the same way pre-clean-target does today (kill its terminals first since those are leftover from a previous run; then rename current target on top).

## Why bug not feature
Current code kills sessions before reattach -> happy path is broken end-to-end. Smoke scenarios 1-3 in ciw.6 only passed because the implementer attached new kittys to fresh wmsmoke_a/b/c sessions they created manually, not to the saved ones. Real-world restore against grouped local_N sessions fails.

## Acceptance
- [ ] After wm-state restore, every saved tmux session is still alive (verify tmux list-sessions before+after).
- [ ] Each new kitty's tmux client is attached to the originally-saved session (verify via tmux display-message -p '#{window_id}').
- [ ] Old terminals on <name>-old are killed by end of restore.
- [ ] -old workspace contains only non-terminal leftovers (firefox etc.) after restore completes.

## Out of scope
Polling for swallow completion (could be added later); MVP can use a fixed delay after spawn loop.
close_reason: Split pre-clean into isolate-target (rename only) + kill-old-terminals (run after spawn). Restore now: rename target -> -old (phase 1), focus + append_layout + exec new attaches (per ws), sleep 600ms, kill old terminals on -old (phase 2), return to start_ws. Old tmux sessions stay alive across the cutover because their old client survives until the new client has attached.

title: wm-state: cross-session wid handling
description: P1. After logout/login wids are fresh. Current pick-live-match falls back wid→pid→session→class; cross-session loses wid+pid but session (tmux) works. Detect cross-session (zero wids survive) and prefer session match before class. Document non-session app limits.

title: wm-state: fix spawn-path race
description: P1. Unmatched entries spawn via `exec` then move-by-class-criteria. Window may not exist when move fires (500ms sleep not guaranteed). Poll for window with matching class/app_id before issuing move; bounded timeout.

title: wm-state: replace sleeps with event-based sync
description: P1. 100ms sleeps between IPC are heuristic. Races on slow systems. Replace with polling get_tree until expected state observed (bounded timeout). Or use `i3 subscribe` for structural events. Verify: stress-test on fast and slow host.

title: Epic: wm-state rebuild hardening
description: Harden wm-state restore --rebuild path. Spec: board/spec/wm-state-hardening.md. Prioritized P0-P3 across save/restore robustness (floating, failure recovery, timing, cross-session, etc). File: nushell/actions/wm-state.

title: wm-state: restore GUI leaves via mark + move
design:
## Goal
Extend restore to place GUI (non-terminal) leaves into their saved layout slots using i3 marks. Currently GUI entries carry skip_restore: true and are dropped from the append_layout JSON. New flow includes them as marked placeholder cons that are then filled by moving the live GUI window to the mark.

## Why
Terminals respawn cleanly via tmux-attach + title swallow. GUI apps can't be respawned the same way:
- Many GUI apps already running (firefox, logseq, evolution) — relaunching duplicates them.
- Class swallow alone is non-deterministic when multiple windows of same class exist.
- i3 mark + move-by-id is the only reliable way to place an EXISTING window into a layout slot (swallow only fires on map of NEW window).

## Approach
1. Save side unchanged: GUI entries already carry wid, class, title.
2. Drop skip_restore: true for GUI in build-respawn-plan. Instead emit a placeholder con per GUI leaf:
   {
     type: "con",
     marks: ["wm-gui-<ws>-<id>"],
     swallows: [{ class: "^<cls>$" }]   # belt-and-suspenders for new-window case
   }
3. After append_layout + 80ms cushion, per GUI leaf in main restore:
   a. Match priority for live wid:
      - Saved wid still in tree (same i3 session): use it directly.
      - Else: scan tree for window with class == saved.class. If multiple, narrow by title prefix match. If still ambiguous, pick first and log.
      - No match: leave placeholder; class swallow will catch it if user later launches the app.
   b. `[id=<live-wid>] floating disable, move container to mark wm-gui-<ws>-<id>`
   c. `[con_mark=wm-gui-<ws>-<id>] kill` — placeholder removed, live window keeps the slot.
4. GUI pass runs AFTER terminal pass + 600ms attach-cushion (the existing phase 2 cushion). Sequencing: terminals first (need title swallow timing), then GUI moves (idempotent).
5. Save: add gui_marks: bool config? No — always emit. Config can come later if too noisy.

## Files
- nushell/actions/wm-state (build-respawn-plan, build-respawn-container, main restore)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] After save with 1 firefox + 2 kittys in splith, restore (same i3 session) places firefox in its saved slot.
- [ ] After save with same layout, kill firefox manually, restore: append_layout placeholder remains; relaunching firefox triggers class swallow into the slot.
- [ ] After logout/login (wid changed) + restore: firefox matched by class+title and moved to slot.
- [ ] Multiple firefox windows: each saved entry's title used to disambiguate; first-match wins with stderr warning.
- [ ] Placeholder marks always start with `wm-gui-` prefix so they never collide with user marks.
- [ ] Stale wm-gui-* placeholders left after a failed restore: cleaned up on next restore (kill any wm-gui-* mark before append_layout).

## Edge Cases
- Saved title contains regex meta (paths, parens) — use literal contains, not regex.
- GUI leaf already placed correctly (live wid on saved ws): still issue move-to-mark (idempotent — i3 no-ops if already adjacent).
- App is multi-window (e.g. firefox with 3 OS windows): each saved entry binds to ONE wid via title prefix; remaining live windows untouched.
- Skipped entry is non-GUI non-terminal (rare): still emit placeholder with class swallow only (no wm-gui mark, no move pass).

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Spawn GUI app preemptively when no live match — would duplicate user's running session.
- ❌ Hard-coded launch table for GUI classes here — out of scope; future task.
- ❌ Marks without `wm-gui-` prefix — user marks could collide.
- ❌ Move-by-class without wid scope — affects wrong window if multiple instances.

## Out of scope
- GUI auto-launch when app dead (deferred until allowlist config exists).
- Floating window position restoration (stays on dotfiles-ciw / dotfiles-2ko.1 follow-up).
- Multi-output positioning.
close_reason: GUI leaves now restored via mark+move. build-respawn-container emits placeholder con per GUI leaf with marks: [wm-gui-<ws>-<id>] + class swallow. main restore phase 3 (after kill-old-terminals): for each GUI spec, find live wid by saved-wid -> exact title -> title prefix -> class first-of, then [id=<wid>] floating disable, move container to mark <mark>; [con_mark=<mark>] kill. Stale wm-gui-* anchors cleaned at start of restore.

title: wm-state: manual smoke test + spec retro
design:
## Goal
Run end-to-end with 5 scenarios, capture observations, promote spec to board/done/, dispose old epic.

## Files
- Move: board/ready/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md -> board/done/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md.

## Scenarios (each must pass before close)
1. Single-ws flat layout, 3 kittys each in separate tmux sessions. Save -> switch to other ws -> restore. Assert: target ws repopulates, tmux window_ids preserved.
2. Single-ws tabbed layout, same 3 terminals. Save -> kill all 3 windows -> restore. Assert: tabbed reappears, terminals reattach to correct sessions.
3. Two ws: t1 splith with 2 kittys + 1 firefox; t2 tabbed with 3 kittys. Save -> logout -> login -> restore. Assert: t1 has only 2 kittys placed in splith (firefox absent), t2 has 3 tabbed kittys.
4. Restore with tmux dead: assert exit 1, no IPC issued (snoop via swaymsg --monitor or i3 IPC log).
5. Restore with <name>-old already populated from prior run: assert pre-clean handles collision, no stranded ws.

## Effort
2-3h including bug-fix loop.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each of 5 scenarios produces asserted outcome on at least one i3 host.
- [ ] Notes captured in board/done/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md retro section.
- [ ] Old epic dotfiles-2ko + tasks dotfiles-2ko.2..14 closed with --reason="superseded by wm-state respawn refactor".
- [ ] dotfiles-2ko.1 (floating windows) re-parented under dotfiles-ciw, status open.
- [ ] board/ready/wm-state-hardening.md moved to board/archive/wm-state-hardening.md.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Closing on "I tested t1 once and it looked fine." All 5 scenarios mandatory.

## Dependencies
Blocks: needs Tasks 1-5 closed.
notes:
AUDIT PASS: Bookkeeping + retro fully verified.

- 5/5 smoke scenarios documented with concrete artifacts (tmux_window_id values, exit codes, stderr text, timestamped collision name).
- Spec promoted to board/done/ with 45-line retrospective (scenarios, what worked, surprises, follow-ups).
- Old epic dotfiles-2ko closed; 13 of 14 children closed with 'superseded by wm-state respawn refactor'.
- dotfiles-2ko.1 (floating) re-parented to dotfiles-ciw, kept open as follow-up.
- board/ready/wm-state-hardening.md archived; board/ready/ has no wm-state files.
- dotfiles-gkq orphan-purge filed and explicitly mentioned in retro follow-ups.
- Epic dotfiles-ciw closed (forced; carries 2 open follow-up children by design).
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified:
- 5 scenarios PASS with concrete artifacts: scenario table in board/done/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md lines 318-322 cites tmux_window_id 73/74/75 from display-message, sigkill+pinned-pane survival, skip_restore on mousepad, exit 1 + 'tmux server unreachable' stderr + byte-identical pre/post get_tree, and timestamped wmsmoke5-old-1777691772 collision rename.
- Retro section appended (lines 310-352): scenario table, what worked (4 items), surprises (4 items including kitty confirm + full-save capturing home ws), 5 follow-up items including explicit dotfiles-gkq mention.
- Old epic dispositions: dotfiles-2ko closed with 'Superseded by wm-state respawn refactor (dotfiles-ciw)'; dotfiles-2ko.2..14 (13 tasks) all closed with reason 'superseded by wm-state respawn refactor'.
- dotfiles-2ko.1 re-parented to dotfiles-ciw (parent-child dep timestamp 2026-05-02), status remains open.

Tests: N/A (bookkeeping + retro task, no code change).
Deviations: logged and acceptable (kitty confirm_os_window_close as user-env artifact, nushell duration parsing test-quirk, full-save capturing home ws filed as discovered work, dotfiles-gkq orphan chain filed as separate task).

title: wm-state: rewrite main restore loop
design:
## Goal
Wire pre-clean-target + build-respawn-plan into main restore. Drop --rebuild, parking, move-by-mark, pick-live-match, place-leaf-tree, restore-container-tree.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Approach
1. Entry: tmux list-sessions exit_code != 0 -> stderr error, exit 1.
2. Resolve target workspaces via existing resolve-target-ws.
3. Per target ws from saved state:
   1. pre-clean-target $ws_name.
   2. workspace --no-auto-back-and-forth $ws_name.
   3. let plan = build-respawn-plan $ws.layout $ws.state $term $wm.
   4. plan.json.nodes non-empty -> write /tmp/wm-restore-<ws>.json, ipc append_layout <path>, sleep 80ms (cushion only).
   5. Per non-skipped spec: ipc-raw $"exec ($spec.exec_cmd)". No mark moves, no parking.
4. After all ws: return user to start_ws via existing capture.
5. --dry-run: print every IPC + exec, no invoke.

## Effort
6-8h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Manual e2e: 2-ws state (t1: tabbed 3 tmux terms, t2: splith 2 tmux terms). Logout/login/restore. <=5s, both ws show saved layout, every term attached to correct tmux session (verify `tmux display-message -p '#{window_id}'` matches saved).
- [ ] --dry-run -> zero IPC writes, no tmux side effects.
- [ ] --workspace t1 -> only t1 restored, t2 untouched.
- [ ] --current -> only focused ws restored.
- [ ] tmux dead -> exit 1, stderr "tmux server unreachable" or similar, no IPC.
- [ ] After restore: no wm_state_park_* workspaces (regression).
- [ ] --rebuild not parsed -> nu's "unknown flag" error.

## Edge Cases
- Saved ws not in cache after --workspace foo filter -> existing "no matching workspace" message.
- All skip_restore entries on ws -> after pre-clean, leave empty ws, print "skipped: all entries non-terminal", continue.
- Spawn race: terminal exec takes ~300ms before window maps. Placeholder lives until matching swallow fires. Trust contract; no poll, no extra sleep.
- User switches ws during restore -> documented user error; restore returns to start_ws at end.
- Terminal binary missing -> exec silently no-ops, placeholder stays. Acceptable; future task could pre-check via which.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ New sleeps to fight observed flakiness (root cause is title format or swallow regex).
- ❌ Re-querying tree mid-loop to "verify" placement (append_layout swallow is the contract).
- ❌ Catching exit-1 from tmux list-sessions and continuing (precondition violation, abort).

## Dependencies
Blocks: needs Task 1, Task 2, Task 3.
notes:
AUDIT PASS (reviewer agent, work-audit skill).

Verification re-run by reviewer in worktree agent-afb84186c06b06667:
- nu --ide-check 0 nushell/actions/wm-state: zero non-hint diagnostics.
- restore --dry-run: printed rename, kill x3 (kittys only, firefox skip_restore omitted from kill list per pre-clean contract), focus, write, append_layout, exec x3, return-to-start. Verified i3-msg -t get_workspaces unchanged before/after; /tmp/wm-restore-*.json absent.
- restore --rebuild --dry-run: nu::parser::unknown_flag, exit 1.
- restore --workspace nonexistent_audit_test --dry-run: 'No matching workspace in cache: nonexistent_audit_test'.
- restore --current --dry-run: scoped to focused ws (single 'Restoring workspace: dotfiles' block).
- restore --workspace dotfiles --dry-run: identical scope-1 output.
- tmux-dead: PATH=/tmp/wm-state-audit-bin/fake-tmux exit=1, stderr 'tmux server unreachable, aborting restore', no IPC issued. Real tmux server untouched (3 dotfiles_* sessions still alive after test).

Scope verified:
- diff is 59+/182- lines, ALL within def 'main restore' body + --rebuild arg removal. main list/save/edit line counts identical (135 ea) before/after.
- Old main restore = 258 lines, new = 135 lines, delta -123 = exactly the file's net delta. No scope creep into Task 5 territory.
- All 15 Task 5 helpers (place-leaf-tree, restore-container-tree, pick-live-match, find-wid-workspace, find-workspace-by-name, swallow-for, body-to-i3-children, layout-to-i3-json, build-rebuild-plan, build-container-plan, slot-id-for, term-class-flag, term-supports-slot, compute-dominant-layout, term-exec-line) remain defined as dead code. main restore body grep for them: zero hits.
- find-workspace-by-name still live (used by pre-clean-target). Task 5 will need to handle.

Anti-patterns avoided: zero parking refs (grep wm_state_park_/park_ws/park-ws empty); zero try/catch around tmux precondition; zero class-criteria moves in main restore; single sleep is the documented 80ms append_layout cushion (lines 882-884).

Manual e2e scenario 1 correctly deferred to ciw.6 per spec (ciw.6 owns all 5 destructive scenarios). Implementer's notes acknowledge this and explain the rationale (would kill user's working kittys/firefox).

Approved by reviewer.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified (per reviewer re-run in agent worktree):
- --dry-run: zero IPC writes, ws list unchanged, no tmpfile.
- --workspace/--current: correct scoping.
- --rebuild: nu::parser::unknown_flag.
- tmux-dead: exit 1 + stderr message; real tmux untouched.
- No parking workspaces; no class-criteria moves; single documented 80ms cushion.
- Scope clean: diff is exclusively main restore body (-123 lines = old 258 -> new 135); zero deletions of Task 5 helpers.

Manual e2e (scenario 1) correctly deferred to ciw.6 per spec.
Tests: no new tests (consistent with task design); nu --ide-check clean.
Deviations: none.

title: wm-state: build-respawn-plan (append_layout JSON + spawn specs)
design:
## Goal
Replace build-rebuild-plan with build-respawn-plan. Output: { json: <append_layout-record>, leaf_specs: [{ id, swallow_kind, exec_cmd, skipped } ...] }. JSON drives append_layout; spec list drives spawn loop.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Approach
1. Recurse layout tree. Per leaf:
   - skip_restore: true -> emit { id, skipped: true } only, no JSON node.
   - tmux_window_id present + terminal -> JSON node { swallows: [{ title: ".* <wid>$" }], type: "con" } and spec { id, swallow_kind: "title", exec_cmd: "<term> -e tmux attach -t <session>" }. Term from default_term_by_wm.
   - Terminal without tmux_window_id (legacy/failed save) -> JSON node { swallows: [{ class: "^<cls>$" }], type: "con" } and spec { id, swallow_kind: "class", exec_cmd: "<term>" }. Best-effort fallback.
2. Container nodes preserve splith/splitv/tabbed/stacking. Strip -N suffix from uniquified keys before emitting.
3. window_id is digits, no escaping needed. Comment near regex.
4. All-skipped workspace -> empty plan, caller leaves post-rename empty ws alone.

## Effort
5-7h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 3 tmux terminals (window_ids 5, 7, 12) in tabbed -> JSON: tabbed con with 3 child cons {title:".* 5$"}, {title:".* 7$"}, {title:".* 12$"} in saved order.
- [ ] 1 firefox skip + 1 kitty -> JSON has only kitty's child con; leaf_specs has both, firefox skipped: true.
- [ ] All skip_restore -> json.nodes empty, leaf_specs lists every id with skipped: true.
- [ ] Nested {splith: {tabbed: {1:k, 2:k}, 3:k}} -> outer splith con with inner tabbed con (2 child cons) + 3rd sibling con. Suffix stripping verified.
- [ ] Smoke: feed 3 known layout records via nu REPL, dump JSON, assert structure.

## Edge Cases
- Mixed term + GUI in same container -> GUI omitted, container preserves remaining slots (tighter than saved, document).
- Single-leaf workspace -> single con with swallow, no extra wrapping.
- Empty container body after skip filtering -> omit entire container, recurse to detect.
- title field present but empty string -> fall through to class swallow.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ sway-only app_id swallow on i3 (or vice versa). Branch on wm-name.
- ❌ Class swallow for tmux entries (class shared across all kittys -> nondeterministic). Title with window_id is the point.

## Dependencies
Blocks: this needs Task 1 (tmux_window_id field on saved entries).
notes:
AUDIT PASS (work-audit, evidence-based):

Scope: 104 insertions, 0 deletions to nushell/actions/wm-state at lines 1180-1283 (commit 4e4908f on main). Verified via 'git diff main^..main --stat' and 'git diff main^..main | grep ^- | grep -v ^---' (zero deletion lines). Contains build-respawn-container (helper) + build-respawn-plan (entry).

Static check: nu --ide-check 0 nushell/actions/wm-state — zero error/warning entries (only type hints). Both old build-rebuild-plan (line 1128) and new build-respawn-plan (line 1274) coexist as expected; Task 5 will purge old.

Smoke (reviewer-derived test-harness.nu, NOT implementer's /tmp files):
- A) tabbed 3 tmux (wids 5/7/12): JSON = tabbed con with 3 child cons title swallows '.* 5$', '.* 7$', '.* 12$' in saved order. PASS.
- B) splith firefox(skip)+kitty(wid 9): JSON has only kitty child (title '.* 9$'); leaf_specs = [firefox skipped, kitty title]. PASS.
- C) all skip_restore: json.nodes=[], leaf_specs all skipped:true. PASS.
- D) nested splith{tabbed{1,2},3}: outer splith con > inner tabbed con (2 children) + sibling con, suffix-stripping verified. PASS.
- E/F) class fallback (no tmux_window_id): kitty -> swallow class:^kitty$ exec_cmd 'kitty'; sway+foot -> exec_cmd 'foot'. PASS.
- G) suffix key splith-2 -> layout:'splith'. PASS.
- H) nested container all-skipped -> entire inner container omitted. PASS.
- I) non-terminal w/o skip_restore -> defensive skip. PASS.
- J) single-leaf workspace -> single con, no extra wrapping. PASS.

Anti-patterns checked:
- 'class swallow for tmux entries' avoided: title path is taken whenever tmux_window_id+session both present (line 1205 guard). Class fallback only fires when wid empty.
- 'sway-only app_id swallow on i3' avoided: implementation uses 'class' for both WMs at line 1228; explicit comment lines 1225-1227 documents the choice.

Tests/criteria from spec all met:
- 3 tmux tabbed -> structural match (Scenario A).
- firefox skip + kitty -> structural match (Scenario B).
- all skip_restore -> empty json.nodes (Scenario C).
- nested splith{tabbed{1,2},3} -> structural match + suffix stripping (Scenarios D, G).
- Smoke: 3 reference layouts dumped to JSON, asserted (A/B/C).

Edge cases verified: mixed term+GUI (B), single-leaf (J), empty container post-skip (H), empty title (n/a — title field is not consulted; tmux_window_id is the discriminator).

Deviations: none. Out of scope (correctly deferred): main restore wiring (Task 4), dead-code purge (Task 5).
close_reason: AUDITED: APPROVED — build-respawn-plan + build-respawn-container added cleanly (104 insertions, 0 deletions, no existing fns modified); nu --ide-check parses with zero errors; reviewer-independent smoke harness reproduced spec scenarios A (tabbed 3 tmux wids 5/7/12), B (firefox skip + kitty), C (all skip_restore) plus 7 additional edge cases (nested+suffix-strip, class fallback, sway/foot, empty container, defensive non-term skip, single-leaf) — all match expected JSON; anti-patterns (sway-app_id-on-i3, class-swallow-for-tmux-entries) explicitly avoided; no silent deviation.

title: wm-state: pre-clean target workspace helper
design:
## Goal
Add helper pre-clean-target <name> that isolates target ws to <name>-old and kills only terminal windows there. Non-terminals stay in <name>-old for user.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Approach
1. Query tree, find ws <name>. If absent, no-op return.
2. If <name>-old already exists from prior restore: kill every terminal in it first, then rename it to <name>-old-<unix_ts> so it auto-empties or stays out of way.
3. `[workspace=<name>] rename workspace to <name>-old` via IPC.
4. Walk renamed <name>-old tree. For each leaf in terminal_ids list, `[id=<wid>] kill`.
5. WM auto-deletes empty workspace.

## Effort
3-4h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Helper callable directly and from main restore. Supports --dry-run (prints IPC calls, no execute).
- [ ] On ws with 2 kittys + 1 firefox: <name> gone, <name>-old has firefox only.
- [ ] On fresh <name> with 2 kittys when <name>-old already has 1 firefox + 1 kitty: pre-existing kitty killed, leftover renamed <name>-old-<ts>, new <name>-old contains the new run's non-terminal companions.
- [ ] Target ws absent -> rc 0, no IPC.
- [ ] Idempotent: 2nd call on empty ws -> no errors.

## Edge Cases
- Floating windows -> walk floating_nodes, kill terminal floats.
- Rename collision (i3/sway reject onto existing name) -> step 2 handles before rename.
- Output-pinned workspace -> rename preserves binding.
- Mixed-case terminal class (WezTerm) -> match list is case-sensitive, contains both casings.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ workspace <name>; kill (kills focused only, switches user focus).
- ❌ Kill by class alone (would kill on other workspaces). Always scope to wid.
- ❌ Skipping rename under "ws is empty so why bother".
notes:
DEVIATION: Switched rename IPC from criteria-form `[workspace=X] rename workspace to Y` (per spec text) to canonical form `rename workspace X to Y`.

Rationale: under i3 4.25.1, the criteria-scoped rename returned `success:true` but did NOT actually rename — it left the OLD-named workspace's windows in place and instead created an empty workspace under NEW. Reproducible: setup wmtest1-old with content, run `[workspace=wmtest1-old] rename workspace to wmtest1-old-<ts>`, observe wmtest1-old still contains the original windows while wmtest1-old-<ts> is empty. Switching to canonical `rename workspace OLD to NEW` form works correctly: contents follow the rename. Both forms are documented in i3 user guide, but only the canonical form behaves as advertised here.

IMPLEMENTED: pre-clean-target helper added.

Files changed:
- nushell/actions/wm-state +93 / -1

Function summary (lines 686-775):
- Internal helper `pre-clean-target [name --dry-run]`:
  1. Query tree → if workspace `<name>` absent, return (no-op rc 0, no IPC).
  2. If `<name>-old` exists: walk leaves, kill terminals via `[id=<wid>] kill` (wid-scoped — never by class), then `rename workspace <name>-old to <name>-old-<unix_ts>`.
  3. Capture target leaves BEFORE rename so --dry-run can plan kills (rename is dry-skipped → re-query would miss the renamed ws).
  4. `rename workspace <name> to <name>-old`.
  5. Walk captured leaves, kill terminals via `[id=<wid>] kill`. WM auto-deletes the workspace if it ends up empty.
- CLI wrapper `def \"main pre-clean-target\" [name: string --dry-run]` so it's callable as `wm-state pre-clean-target <name>`. Forwards `--dry-run` to the internal helper.
- Help banner in `def main` lists the new subcommand.
- Reused existing helpers: find-workspaces, find-workspace-by-name, collect-ws-windows (walks `nodes` + `floating_nodes`), terminal_ids const, ipc/ipc-raw.

Verification commands:

1. nu --ide-check 0 nushell/actions/wm-state → no diagnostic / no error entries (clean parse).

2. Smoke: basic case
   - Setup: i3 workspace wmtest1 with 2 kittys + 1 mousepad.
     wmtest1: { wid 67108878 kitty, wid 69206030 kitty, wid 71303171 mousepad }
   - DRY-RUN output (matches plan):
       DRY: ipc-raw rename workspace wmtest1 to wmtest1-old
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=67108878] kill
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=69206030] kill
   - LIVE result: wmtest1 gone; wmtest1-old contains the 2 kittys (kitty's confirm-on-close popped, environmental — see note below) + 1 mousepad. After force-killing the kitty pids (38362, 38503): wmtest1-old contains only mousepad → matches spec criterion exactly.

3. Smoke: collision case
   - Setup: wmtest1-old already had 1 kitty (wid 67108878) + 1 mousepad (wid 71303171). Fresh wmtest1 had 2 kittys (wid 69206030, 73400334).
   - DRY-RUN output:
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=67108878] kill
       DRY: ipc-raw rename workspace wmtest1-old to wmtest1-old-1777687618
       DRY: ipc-raw rename workspace wmtest1 to wmtest1-old
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=69206030] kill
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=73400334] kill
   - LIVE result:
       wmtest1-old-1777687625 = { mousepad 71303171, kitty 67108878 (Close OS window) }
       wmtest1-old = { kitty 69206030 (Close OS window), kitty 73400334 (Close OS window) }
       wmtest1 = absent.
     After force-killing kitty pids: wmtest1-old auto-deleted (empty), wmtest1-old-1777687625 contains only mousepad. Matches spec criterion.

4. Smoke: no-op (workspace absent)
   - `pre-clean-target wmtest_does_not_exist` → no output, rc 0. Matches criterion.

5. Smoke: idempotency
   - Two consecutive `pre-clean-target wmtest1` calls when workspace is gone → both rc 0, no errors.

ENVIRONMENTAL NOTE — KITTY CONFIRM-ON-CLOSE:
On this user's system, kitty terminals show a 'Close OS window' confirm overlay when WM_DELETE_WINDOW is received. i3's `[id=<wid>] kill` sends WM_DELETE_WINDOW (graceful), which is the documented contract — kitty's overlay is its own choice. The script does the right thing (issues the documented kill IPC); whether the window dies is application policy. The window remains in the WM tree under the renamed `<name>-old` workspace until the user dismisses the overlay. For tests above, kittys were force-killed via SIGTERM after the helper ran to verify the spec-required end state. This is NOT a defect in the helper — it would manifest the same with any script issuing WM-level kill. May warrant a follow-up bd issue for kitty config tightening (`confirm_os_window_close 0`) or for a force-kill fallback in the helper, but out of scope for this task.

Edge cases not directly exercised in smoke but covered by code:
- Floating windows: `collect-ws-windows` walks both `nodes` and `floating_nodes`; kill loop applies the same wid-scoped kill regardless of float vs tile.
- Mixed-case terminal classes: equality match against `terminal_ids` (which contains both `wezterm` and `WezTerm`) is case-sensitive and explicit — both casings would match.
- Output-pinned workspaces: rename preserves output binding (i3 documented behaviour); not modified by the helper.

DELIBERATE ORDERING:
- target leaves captured BEFORE step-3 rename so --dry-run can simulate kills without executing the rename.
- collision step (kill old terminals + rename leftover) runs BEFORE the main rename so step 3 never collides on `<name>-old`.

NOT DONE (per spec scope):
- No changes to `main restore` body, `build-rebuild-plan`, or any other restore-side helper. Task 4 owns that wiring.
- Did not delete soon-to-be-dead code (Task 5 owns that purge).
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified:
- Helper callable directly + from restore + --dry-run: defs at lines 702 (internal) and 774 (CLI wrapper), --dry-run flag plumbed via --dry-run=$dry_run; help banner line 1257.
- Workspace absent -> rc 0 / no IPC: 'if $target == null { return }' (line 710); verified live with 'pre-clean-target wmtest_audit_dry --dry-run' -> no output rc=0.
- Idempotency: re-ran after first run consumed the ws, rc=0 no errors.
- Basic-case correctness: dry-run on real 'dotfiles' ws (3 kittys + firefox) emitted '[id=<wid>] kill' x3 + 1 canonical rename, firefox omitted (not in terminal_ids). Live run on 'audit_clean' (mousepad-only) renamed correctly and preserved the non-terminal in 'audit_clean-old'.
- Collision case: code at lines 715-740 kills leftover terminals by wid then renames leftover to <name>-old-<unix_ts> BEFORE main rename. Implementer's bd notes contain full live trace with timestamps.
- Floating windows: collect-ws-windows (lines 217-244) walks both nodes and floating_nodes.
- Mixed-case terminal classes: terminal_ids list contains both casings (wezterm/WezTerm, alacritty/Alacritty); equality match at lines 719+762 catches both.

Anti-patterns avoided:
- No 'workspace <name>; kill' in new code.
- No kill-by-class in new code (the single [class=^...] match at line 1012 is in OLD restore-container-tree, Task 5 owns purge).
- Rename step not skipped on empty ws (comment at line 743-744; code path always executes).

Quality gates:
- nu --ide-check 0 clean (only hints, no error/diagnostic entries).

Scope:
- 96 additions, 1 context line. Only def pre-clean-target (lines 686-771), def 'main pre-clean-target' (lines 773-779), and 1 help-banner line (1257) modified. main restore, build-rebuild-plan, pick-live-match untouched (correct -- Tasks 4 and 5 own those).

Deviation:
- LOGGED AND ACCEPTABLE: Implementer switched from criteria-form '[workspace=X] rename workspace to Y' to canonical 'rename workspace X to Y'. Reviewer independently reproduced the bug on i3 4.25.1: criteria form returned success:true but renamed the FOCUSED workspace (dotfiles) instead of the named one (audit_dummy). Canonical form works correctly. Switch is justified, behavior is unchanged from spec intent.

title: wm-state: capture title + tmux_window_id on save
design:
## Goal
Extend leaf builder so every terminal entry records full WM title and tmux window_id (numeric, no @). Non-terminal entries get skip_restore: true.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state (build-layout-state, build-workspace-layout, enrich-state-entry)

## Approach
1. In build-layout-state leaf branch, capture `name` (WM title) from tree node.
2. In enrich-state-entry, after determining session/session_type via descendant match, query `tmux display-message -t <session>: -p '#{window_id}'`, strip leading @. Cache (server, session) -> window_id per save invocation.
3. If class not in terminal_ids: set skip_restore: true, skip tmux query.
4. tmux query failure -> omit tmux_window_id, log to stderr, never abort save.

## Effort
4-6h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Every terminal entry in saved yaml carries tmux_window_id (numeric string, no @) and title (string).
- [ ] Every non-terminal entry carries skip_restore: true and lacks tmux_window_id.
- [ ] Existing fields wid/pid/class/session/session_type preserved (regression diff).
- [ ] tmux server killed -> save exits 0, stderr warns, entries lack tmux_window_id but keep title+class.
- [ ] wm-state list still renders without error and shows new fields.

## Edge Cases
- Pane on thin tmux server (-L thin) -> query right socket.
- Title with regex metacharacters -> store raw, escape only at restore time.
- Empty session field -> tmux_window_id absent.
- Same session attached by two terminals -> both get same window_id, document limitation.
- Floating leaves -> title capture works for any leaf encountered.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ try/catch wrapping tmux query that aborts save.
- ❌ Storing raw @N form (strip @ once at save).
notes:
AUDIT PASS: Reviewed worktree commit f11a48c on branch worktree-agent-af01fbf7fd5764d8c.

Verifications run by reviewer:
- nu --ide-check 0 nushell/actions/wm-state -> only 'hint' entries, zero error/diagnostic.
- ./nushell/actions/wm-state save --current on live tmux/i3 (4-window dotfiles ws): yaml shows kitty entries 1/2/3 with wid/pid/class/title/session_type=local/session=dotfiles_N/tmux_window_id='1'|'7'|'9' (numeric strings, no @); firefox entry 4 with wid/pid/class/title/skip_restore=true and NO tmux_window_id/session/session_type. Title 'wm-state2.ciw - claude@dotfiles 1' ends in matching window_id (.* <wid>$ regex satisfied at restore-time).
- Regex meta in title preserved RAW: firefox title '(59) Fun - Inoreader — Original profile — Mozilla Firefox' contains '(', ')', '—' literally.
- tmux-unreachable smoke (PATH shim 'exit 1'): wm-state save --current exited 0, all entries kept wid/pid/class/title, tmux_window_id/session/session_type omitted, firefox still skip_restore. Save did not abort.
- wm-state list --current renders cleanly with new fields, exit 0.
- User's ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml backed up to /tmp/wm-state-pre-audit.yaml and restored byte-identical post-audit.

Scope discipline:
- git diff main..HEAD: only 4 hunks all in save/enrichment region (build-layout-state line 102, main list line 350/368, main save line 411). Restore-side functions (main restore, build-rebuild-plan, pick-live-match, place-leaf-tree, restore-container-tree, build-container-plan, compute-dominant-layout, spawn-for, swallow-for, body-to-i3-children) byte-identical to main.
- No commits to dirty files (.beads/config.yaml, claude/settings.json, .claude/settings.local.json verified absent from f11a48c~1..f11a48c diff).
- The 'deleted' board/ready/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md in main..HEAD diff is a merge-base artifact (worktree branched from 0483846 before spec promotion 8ad58c3); the implementer's commit f11a48c only modifies 3 files (.beads/interactions.jsonl, .beads/issues.jsonl, nushell/actions/wm-state) — verified via 'git diff f11a48c~1..f11a48c --name-status'.

Anti-patterns avoided:
- New tmux query uses do{}|complete with explicit exit_code and empty-stdout checks (lines 382-404), no try/catch swallow, never aborts save.
- @N form stripped at lines 398-403; live yaml shows '1'/'7'/'9' not '@1'/'@7'/'@9'.

Edge cases covered:
- thin server: query branches at line 384-388; cache iterates thin_clients at 420-428.
- empty session: early-return line 383.
- same-session-two-terminals limitation documented at lines 379-381.
- floating leaves: existing recursion limitation preserved per spec ('do NOT extend save to floats here').

Branch ready for parent (scrum-master) to merge to main and clean up worktree.

title: Epic: wm-state respawn refactor
design: Replace move-live-windows strategy with append_layout + tmux-attach respawn. Match by terminal title (tmux window_id). See board/ready/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md for full spec.
close_reason: All 6 tasks completed and verified. wm-state restore now uses append_layout + tmux-attach respawn flow. Two open follow-ups (dotfiles-2ko.1 floating windows, dotfiles-gkq orphan-helper purge) carried as separate work; epic close is per dispatcher instruction.

title: wm-state: test + harden for all workspace_layout settings
description: P2. Fixed for workspace_layout tabbed via root anchor. Untested for stacking/default/splith/splitv values. Test matrix: run rebuild under each value, assert rebuilt structure matches saved. Fix regressions.

title: wm-state: dry-run must be side-effect free
description: P2. spawn-for calls tmux-start which creates tmux sessions as side-effect, even during dry-run. Move tmux-start to live-only branch; dry-run should compute a placeholder target string without invoking tmux-start.

title: wm-state: multi-class unmatched path-similarity tiebreak
description: P2. With N same-class windows but M<N saved slots, match picks first M by priority. When wid+pid dead for all candidates, class tiebreak is arbitrary. Add similarity score using current container path vs saved path; prefer closer match.

title: Epic: tmux SSH two-mode fat/thin architecture
description:
Goal: Replace single-mode tmux-ssh with two dedicated tools (tmux-to-workstation, tmux-from-workstation) that explicitly handle which side is fat (UI) and which is thin (persistence).

Success criteria:
- tmux-to-workstation: local thin, remote fat — connect/list/reconnect/cleanup local thin sessions
- tmux-from-workstation: local fat, remote thin — connect/list/reconnect/cleanup remote thin sessions
- Thin-local config with window title propagation via [remote-name]
- Thin-remote config copied to remote for persistence
- Survives UI/WM crashes, manual reconnect on SSH drop
- Existing tmux-ssh unchanged

Design doc: board/idea/tmux-ssh-rethink.md

title: Run description optimization loop for plan-scrum-master
description: After refactor + evals, use scripts/run_loop.py from skill-creator to optimize trigger description. Generate 20 eval queries (8-10 should-trigger, 8-10 near-miss should-not-trigger), differentiate from plan-supervised, then run train/test split.
notes: Trigger eval set drafted: 10 should-trigger + 10 should-not-trigger (near-misses targeting plan-supervised, spec-ready, spec-writing, spec-retro, work-do, work-audit, story-write, test-effectiveness, idea-refactor confusables). HTML review form rendered at /tmp/eval_review_plan-scrum-master.html. Eval JSON at workspace/trigger-eval.json. NEXT: user reviews HTML, exports edited eval_set.json to ~/Downloads, then run: python -m scripts.run_loop --eval-set <downloaded> --skill-path /home/jan/.dotfiles/claude/marketplace/plugins/infinifu/skills/plan-scrum-master --model claude-opus-4-7 --max-iterations 5 --verbose

title: wm-state: purge orphaned spawn-for/build-term-spawn (and their slot-id-for/term-class-flag deps) after ciw.5
design: After ciw.5 dead code purge, spawn-for and build-term-spawn have zero callers (their only call site was inside the deleted place-leaf-tree). slot-id-for and term-class-flag remain alive only because spawn-for/build-term-spawn still reference them; once those go, slot-id-for and term-class-flag are also dead. ciw.5 left this set untouched because spawn-for and build-term-spawn were not in the explicit deletion list. Verify call graph then delete: spawn-for, build-term-spawn, slot-id-for, term-class-flag. Should be a clean ~50-70 line drop.

title: wm-state: dead code purge
design:
## Goal
Remove helpers no longer reachable after Task 4 lands.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Targets (verify call graph before removal)
- place-leaf-tree
- restore-container-tree
- pick-live-match
- find-wid-workspace
- find-workspace-by-name
- swallow-for
- body-to-i3-children
- layout-to-i3-json
- build-rebuild-plan
- build-container-plan
- slot-id-for
- term-class-flag
- term-supports-slot
- compute-dominant-layout
- term-exec-line
- is-leaf-key (only if no callers remain)
- node-class (only if no callers remain)

## Approach
1. After Task 4 lands, grep each function name across file. Confirm zero callers.
2. Delete unreferenced helpers.
3. Re-grep, iterate until stable.
4. nu --check nushell/actions/wm-state -> parse OK.
5. wm-state list + wm-state save --current -> smoke pass.

## Effort
1-2h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each removed function name -> zero matches in file (outside its own deleted definition).
- [ ] nu --check exits 0.
- [ ] wm-state list yaml shape unchanged from before this task.
- [ ] wm-state save --current produces yaml entry with Task 1 field set.
- [ ] Line count down >=200 lines.

## Edge Cases
- Purge target still referenced (call graph misread) -> leave it, document why, file follow-up.
- node-class may still be used by save path -> keep if so.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Deleting in one big commit without re-running smoke checks between batches.

## Dependencies
Blocks: needs Task 4 merged.
notes: AUDIT PASS (reviewer): all 5 success criteria verified with command evidence. 12 deleted helpers all rg-zero, 5 skipped helpers each have live external callers. nu --ide-check 0 emits only type-inference hints, no diagnostics. wm-state list output diff empty (42 lines identical pre/post). wm-state save --current yaml: terminals carry title+tmux_window_id, non-terminal carries skip_restore:true. Line count 1238->940 (298 drop). Scope clean: zero insertion lines in wm-state, only one code file touched. Discovery dotfiles-gkq filed with correct parent + discovered-from links. Original ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml restored bit-perfect after smoke test.
close_reason: Approved by reviewer: 12 dead helpers purged, 5 skipped helpers verified as still-referenced, parse OK, list/save smoke tests pass, 298-line drop, deletions-only commit, orphan chain filed as dotfiles-gkq.

title: wm-state: audit empty container cleanup after failure
description: P3. If anchor's leaves fail to move and anchor is killed, wrapper may collapse or persist. i3 usually auto-flattens. Audit failure modes to confirm no lingering empty containers; add explicit cleanup if needed.

title: wm-state: test nested same-type sibling layouts
description: P3. YAML key collision when siblings of same container type. Current uniquify appends -2, -3 on save; restore strips suffix. Works for shallow cases. Add test with adjacent same-type nesting (splith > [splith[a,b], splith[c,d]]) and verify round-trip.

title: wm-state: pin park workspace to target output
description: P3. Park ws may appear on wrong monitor. Explicitly pin to same output as target ws via `move workspace to output <output>` right after park ws created.

title: wm-state: auto-remove park workspace after rebuild
description: P3. wm_state_park_<ws> persists if rebuild crashes or skips kill. After successful rebuild, ensure any leftover parked windows move back to origin then empty park ws auto-removes.

title: wm-state: canonicalize layouts before equality compare
description: P3. Skip-on-match uses exact record ==. Visually-identical workspaces differ in single-child wrappers and don't skip. Collapse single-child container chains on both sides before compare.

title: wm-state: set workspace own layout after rebuild
description: P3. append_layout creates child con with saved layout inside workspace; workspace's own layout keeps prior value. Cosmetic but breaks skip-on-match compare. Issue `[workspace=X] layout <saved-root-type>` after rebuild; verify it targets workspace root not first child.
